questTitle: O-Level Chemistry: Applications of Electrolysis Worksheet 2
subject: Chemistry
description: Master the applications of electrolysis! This worksheet covers electroplating, extraction of metals, production of sodium hydroxide from brine, purification of metals, mass calculations, and hydrogen fuel cells. Show all working for calculations!

questions.question: Electrolysis has many industrial applications. Which of the following is NOT a common application of electrolysis?
options: Extraction of reactive metals from ores

questions.question: Electroplating is the process of coating one metal with a thin layer of another metal using electrolysis. What are the main purposes of electroplating?
options: To make metals conduct electricity better
To protect from corrosion and improve appearance
questions.answer: To protect from corrosion and improve appearance

questions.question:
In electroplating an iron spoon with silver:

• The object to be plated (iron spoon) is the...
• The plating metal (silver) is the...

Complete the sentence:
options: Iron spoon is cathode, silver is anode
Iron spoon is anode, silver is cathode
questions.answer: Iron spoon is cathode, silver is anode

questions.question: When electroplating an object with silver, what should the electrolyte contain?
options: A solution containing silver ions (e.g., silver nitrate)
questions.answer: A solution containing silver ions (e.g., silver nitrate)

questions.question: During silver plating of an iron spoon, what happens at the cathode (the spoon)?
options: Silver ions gain electrons and deposit as silver metal on the spoon
questions.answer: Silver ions gain electrons and deposit as silver metal on the spoon

questions.question: Write the half-equation for the reaction at the cathode during silver electroplating:
During silver plating, what happens at the anode (the silver electrode)?
options: Silver atoms dissolve by losing electrons, forming silver ions
questions.answer: Silver atoms dissolve by losing electrons, forming silver ions

questions.question: Write the half-equation for the reaction at the anode during silver electroplating:
In electroplating with a pure metal anode (e.g., silver anode for silver plating), what happens to the concentration of metal ions in the electrolyte?
options: Concentration remains constant (ions deposited = ions dissolved)
questions.answer: Concentration remains constant (ions deposited = ions dissolved)

questions.question:
A steel fork is to be electroplated with nickel. Which setup is correct?

| Anode | Cathode | Electrolyte |
options: Steel fork, Nickel, Nickel(II) sulfate solution
Carbon, Steel fork, Nickel(II) sulfate solution
Nickel, Steel fork, Nickel(II) sulfate solution
Steel fork, Nickel, Sodium chloride solution
questions.answer: Nickel, Steel fork, Nickel(II) sulfate solution

questions.question: Electrolysis is used to extract reactive metals from their ores. Why can't reactive metals like aluminium be extracted by reduction with carbon?
options: Carbon reacts with aluminium to form carbides only
Reactive metals have a stronger affinity for oxygen than carbon; carbon cannot reduce their oxides
questions.answer: Reactive metals have a stronger affinity for oxygen than carbon; carbon cannot reduce their oxides

questions.problemSet: TOPIC 2: Extraction of Aluminium - The Process
questions.question: Aluminium is extracted from its ore, bauxite (Al₂O₃). Before electrolysis, the ore is purified and dissolved in molten cryolite (Na₃AlF₆). Why is cryolite used?
options: To provide sodium for the reaction
To change the colour of aluminium
To lower the melting point of aluminium oxide from 2050°C to about 950°C, reducing energy costs
To increase the melting point of aluminium oxide
questions.answer: To lower the melting point of aluminium oxide from 2050°C to about 950°C, reducing energy costs

questions.question: In the electrolysis of aluminium oxide, what are the electrodes made of?

At the cathode during electrolysis of molten aluminium oxide:

Al³⁺ ions are attracted to the cathode.

What is the half-equation at the cathode?

At the anode during electrolysis of molten aluminium oxide:

O²⁻ ions are attracted to the anode.

What is the half-equation at the anode?

questions.problemSet: TOPIC 2: Extraction of Aluminium - Anode Consumption
questions.question: In the extraction of aluminium, the carbon anodes need to be replaced regularly. Why?
options: The anodes dissolve in the electrolyte
Oxygen produced reacts with the hot carbon anodes forming carbon dioxide, wearing them away
questions.answer: Oxygen produced reacts with the hot carbon anodes forming carbon dioxide, wearing them away

questions.problemSet: TOPIC 2: Extraction of Aluminium - Overall Equation
questions.question: What is the overall equation for the electrolysis of aluminium oxide?

questions.problemSet: TOPIC 2: Extraction of Aluminium ⭐ MAIN QUESTION
questions.question:
In the extraction of aluminium, 12 moles of electrons pass through the electrolyte.

Cathode: Al³⁺ + 3e⁻ → Al

How many moles of aluminium are produced?

Hint: 3 mol e⁻ produces 1 mol Al

questions.problemSet: TOPIC 3: Production of Sodium Hydroxide - Chlor-Alkali Process
questions.question: The chlor-alkali process involves the electrolysis of concentrated sodium chloride solution (brine) to produce three important products. What are these products?
In the industrial chlor-alkali process, a membrane or diaphragm separates the cathode and anode compartments. Why is this separation necessary?
options: To prevent chlorine and sodium hydroxide from mixing and reacting
questions.answer: To prevent chlorine and sodium hydroxide from mixing and reacting

questions.question:
At the cathode in the chlor-alkali process:

Na⁺ and H⁺ ions are present. H⁺ is preferentially discharged.

What is the half-equation and product?
options: Both B and D are acceptable
questions.answer: Both B and D are acceptable

questions.question:
At the anode in the chlor-alkali process:

Cl⁻ and OH⁻ ions are present. In concentrated brine, Cl⁻ is preferentially discharged.

What is the half-equation?

questions.problemSet: TOPIC 3: Chlor-Alkali Process - Formation of NaOH
questions.question:
During the chlor-alkali process:

• H⁺ ions are discharged at cathode
• Cl⁻ ions are discharged at anode
• Na⁺ and OH⁻ ions remain in solution

How is sodium hydroxide formed?
options: Chlorine reacts with water to form NaOH
By direct combination of Na and OH at the electrode
Na⁺ ions combine with OH⁻ ions remaining in solution to form NaOH
questions.answer: Na⁺ ions combine with OH⁻ ions remaining in solution to form NaOH

questions.problemSet: TOPIC 3: Chlor-Alkali Process - Uses of Products
questions.question: Which statement correctly describes a use of the products from the chlor-alkali process?
options: Hydrogen is used to make bleach
All products are disposed as waste
Sodium hydroxide is used in soap and paper manufacturing
Chlorine is used as a fuel
questions.answer: Sodium hydroxide is used in soap and paper manufacturing

questions.problemSet: TOPIC 3: Chlor-Alkali Process - Uses of Chlorine
questions.question: Chlorine produced from the chlor-alkali process has many uses. Which is a common use of chlorine?
options: Water purification and making PVC plastic
questions.answer: Water purification and making PVC plastic

questions.question:
In the chlor-alkali process, 4 moles of electrons pass through the electrolyte.

Cathode: 2H⁺ + 2e⁻ → H₂
Anode: 2Cl⁻ → Cl₂ + 2e⁻

How many moles of hydrogen and chlorine are produced?
Electrolytic purification (refining) is used to purify metals like copper. In the purification of copper, what serves as the cathode and anode?
options: Cathode: Pure copper (thin sheet), Anode: Impure copper (block)
Cathode: Impure copper, Anode: Pure copper
questions.answer: Cathode: Pure copper (thin sheet), Anode: Impure copper (block)

questions.question: In the electrolytic purification of copper, what electrolyte is used?

questions.problemSet: TOPIC 4: Purification of Copper - Anode Process
questions.question:
At the anode (impure copper) during purification:

Copper atoms in the impure copper lose electrons and dissolve into the solution.

What is the half-equation?

questions.problemSet: TOPIC 4: Purification of Copper - Cathode Process
questions.question:
At the cathode (pure copper sheet) during purification:

Cu²⁺ ions from solution are deposited as pure copper.

What is the half-equation?

The impure copper anode contains impurities like silver, gold, and zinc. During electrolysis:

• Reactive impurities (e.g., zinc) dissolve but don't deposit at cathode
• Less reactive impurities (e.g., silver, gold) fall to the bottom

What is the material that collects at the bottom called?

questions.problemSet: TOPIC 4: Purification of Copper - Mass Changes
questions.question: During electrolytic purification of copper, what happens to the mass of the electrodes?
options: Anode mass increases, cathode mass decreases
Anode mass decreases, cathode mass increases
questions.answer: Anode mass decreases, cathode mass increases

questions.problemSet: TOPIC 4: Purification of Copper ⭐ MAIN QUESTION
questions.question: During purification of copper, the anode loses 127 g while the cathode gains 127 g (ignoring impurities). If the molar mass of copper is 63.5 g/mol, how many moles of copper were transferred?

The relationship between moles of electrons and moles of product depends on the charge of the ion.

For Cu²⁺ + 2e⁻ → Cu:
If 1 mol of Cu is deposited, how many moles of electrons are required?

For the electrolysis reaction: Ag⁺ + e⁻ → Ag

If 0.2 mol of electrons pass through the solution, what mass of silver is deposited?

(Ar of Ag = 108 g/mol)

During electrolysis of copper(II) sulfate solution, 0.5 mol of electrons pass through.

Cu²⁺ + 2e⁻ → Cu

Step 1: Moles of Cu = 0.5 ÷ 2 = 0.25 mol
Step 2: Mass of Cu = 0.25 × 64 = ?

(Ar of Cu = 64 g/mol)

In the extraction of aluminium:

Al³⁺ + 3e⁻ → Al

If 27 g of aluminium is produced (Ar of Al = 27), how many moles of electrons were used?

Moles of Al = 27/27 = 1 mol
Moles of e⁻ = 1 × 3 = ?

During electrolysis of molten zinc chloride, 13 g of zinc is deposited at the cathode.

Zn²⁺ + 2e⁻ → Zn (Ar of Zn = 65 g/mol)

How many moles of chlorine gas are produced at the anode?

Hint: Moles of Zn = 13/65 = 0.2 mol; electrons used = 0.4 mol
Anode: 2Cl⁻ → Cl₂ + 2e⁻

During electroplating, 3.2 g of copper is deposited on a steel object.

Cu²⁺ + 2e⁻ → Cu (Ar of Cu = 64 g/mol)

At the same time, hydrogen gas is evolved at the cathode of another cell in series.
2H⁺ + 2e⁻ → H₂

What volume of hydrogen is produced at STP? (1 mol of gas = 24 dm³ at STP)

Moles of Cu = 3.2/64 = 0.05 mol
Moles of e⁻ = 0.05 × 2 = 0.1 mol
Moles of H₂ = 0.1/2 = 0.05 mol
A hydrogen fuel cell is a device that converts chemical energy from hydrogen directly into electrical energy. What is the overall reaction in a hydrogen fuel cell?
In a hydrogen fuel cell, hydrogen and oxygen gases are continuously fed to the electrodes. Unlike batteries, fuel cells:
options: Produce electricity as long as fuel (hydrogen) is supplied
Only work at very low temperatures
questions.answer: Produce electricity as long as fuel (hydrogen) is supplied

questions.problemSet: TOPIC 6: Hydrogen Fuel Cells - Electrode Reactions
questions.question:
In an alkaline hydrogen fuel cell:

At the negative electrode (anode): Hydrogen is oxidised.

What is the half-equation?

questions.problemSet: TOPIC 6: Hydrogen Fuel Cells - Electrode Reactions (continued)
questions.question:
In an alkaline hydrogen fuel cell:

At the positive electrode (cathode): Oxygen is reduced.

What is the half-equation?
What is the ONLY product of a hydrogen fuel cell during operation?

questions.problemSet: TOPIC 6: Hydrogen Fuel Cells vs Burning Hydrogen
questions.question: Compare hydrogen fuel cells to directly burning hydrogen for energy. Which statement is TRUE?
options: Fuel cells convert chemical energy directly to electrical energy with higher efficiency than burning
Burning hydrogen is more efficient than fuel cells
Fuel cells require higher temperatures than burning
questions.answer: Fuel cells convert chemical energy directly to electrical energy with higher efficiency than burning

questions.question: Which is an advantage of hydrogen fuel cells over fossil fuels?
options: Hydrogen is easier to store than petrol
Hydrogen fuel cells work better in cold weather
Hydrogen fuel cells produce only water, with no carbon dioxide emissions contributing to climate change
Hydrogen is cheaper to produce than extracting fossil fuels
questions.answer: Hydrogen fuel cells produce only water, with no carbon dioxide emissions contributing to climate change

questions.question: Which additional advantage do hydrogen fuel cells have over fossil fuels?
options: Hydrogen can be extracted from crude oil easily
No sulfur dioxide emissions (no acid rain contribution)
Fuel cells are much cheaper than petrol engines
questions.answer: No sulfur dioxide emissions (no acid rain contribution)

questions.problemSet: TOPIC 6: Challenges of Hydrogen Fuel Cells
questions.question: Despite advantages, hydrogen fuel cells have challenges. Which is a current challenge for hydrogen fuel cell technology?
options: Fuel cells are too large for vehicles
Hydrogen is too dangerous to use
Storage of hydrogen is difficult (requires high pressure or low temperature); production of hydrogen often uses fossil fuels
questions.answer: Storage of hydrogen is difficult (requires high pressure or low temperature); production of hydrogen often uses fossil fuels

questions.problemSet: TOPIC 6: Hydrogen Fuel Cells ⭐ MAIN QUESTION
questions.question:
In a hydrogen fuel cell, 4 moles of electrons flow through the external circuit.

Using: 2H₂ + 4OH⁻ → 4H₂O + 4e⁻ (at negative electrode)

How many moles of hydrogen gas are consumed?

A factory uses electrolysis for multiple processes:

1. Electroplating 50 car parts with chromium
2. Purifying 10 kg of copper
3. Producing sodium hydroxide from brine

Which statement about these processes is INCORRECT?
options: In copper purification, impure copper is the cathode and pure copper is the anode
All three processes use direct current (d.c.) electricity
In brine electrolysis, hydrogen is produced at the cathode and chlorine at the anode
In electroplating, the car part is the cathode and chromium is the anode
questions.answer: In copper purification, impure copper is the cathode and pure copper is the anode
